title: Automated determination of web page rendering performance
abstract: Automated determination of web page rendering performance techniques and systems are described. In one example, a web page including multiple user-interactive elements that can be selected or navigated via user input is received. A frame rendering system is employed to automatically identify the user-interactive elements and generate scripts to simulate user input at the user-interactive elements. The generated scripts are then used to simulate user input at the user-interactive elements. The scripts may be generated to simulate user interaction via a variety of different device types, and under a variety of different network conditions. During the simulation, a rendering performance score for the web page is generated by monitoring rendering performance relative to a threshold rendering rate. Rendering performance may be monitored on an individual basis while simulating user input at different user-interactive elements, such that the rendering performance report includes individual rendering performance scores for each user-interactive element.
claims: Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. A method implemented by at least one computing device, the method comprising: identifying, by the at least one computing device, user-interactive elements that are included in a web page; simulating, by the at least one computing device, user interaction with the user-interactive elements and measuring a number of frames rendered in a timeframe during the simulating; comparing, by the at least one computing device, the measured number of frames rendered in the timeframe to a threshold frame rendering rate for the at least one computing device to determine whether the simulation of user interaction with the user interactive elements provides a satisfactory user experience in which the measured number of frames rendered in the timeframe satisfies the threshold frame rendering rate; and outputting, by the at least one computing device, a rendering performance report indicating whether the satisfactory user experience is provided. 2. The method as described in claim 1, wherein simulating user interaction with the user-interactive elements comprises: generating, for each of the user-interactive elements, a test script for automating user interaction with the user-interactive element; and applying the generated test scripts to the user-interactive elements. 3. The method as described in claim 1, wherein the user-interactive elements comprise at least one of: a vertical scrolling element; a horizontal scrolling element; an overlay element; a list view element; a radio button element; or a drop-down element. 4. The method as described in claim 1, wherein simulating the user interaction with the user-interactive elements is performed automatically and independent of user input. 5. The method as described in claim 1, wherein the simulating comprises determining a rendering performance of one of the user-interactive elements by measuring a number of frames rendered in the timeframe during the simulating user interaction with the one of the user-interactive elements, and wherein the rendering performance report includes information describing the rendering performance for the one of the user-interactive elements. 6. The method as described in claim 1, wherein the threshold frame rendering rate is 60 frames per second. 7. The method as described in claim 1, further comprising identifying that the simulation of user interaction with one of the user-interactive elements fails to satisfy the threshold frame rendering rate and generating the rendering performance report to include information specifying the identified one of the user- interactive elements as failing to satisfy the threshold frame rendering rate. 8. The method as described in claim 1, wherein simulating user interaction with the user-interactive elements comprises simulating user interaction with the user-interactive elements at a first device type and simulating user interaction with the user-interactive elements at a second device type that is different than the first device type, wherein the rendering performance report includes information describing differences between simulating user interaction at the first and second device types. 9. The method as described in claim 1, wherein simulating user interaction with the user-interactive elements comprises simulating user interaction with the user-interactive elements under a first set of network conditions and simulating user interaction with the user-interactive elements under a second set of network conditions that are different than the first set of network conditions, wherein the rendering performance report includes information describing differences between simulating user interaction under the first and second sets of network conditions. 10. A system comprising: a user-interactive element detection module implemented at least partially in hardware of a computing device to receive a web page and identify a plurality of user-interactive elements that are included in the web page; a test generator module implemented at least partially in hardware of the computing device to generate, for each of the plurality of user-interactive elements included in the web page, a test script for simulating user interaction with the user- interactive element; a user interaction testing module implemented at least partially in hardware of the computing device to test rendering performance of the web page by applying the generated test scripts to the plurality of user-interactive elements and measuring a number of frames rendered in a timeframe during the applying; and a rendering performance reporting module implemented at least partially in hardware of the computing device to determine whether the measured number of frames rendered in the timeframe satisfies a threshold frame rendering rate for the computing device and generate a rendering performance report for the web page based on the determination. 11. The system as described in claim 10, wherein the plurality of user- interactive elements comprise a vertical scrolling element and at least one of: a horizontal scrolling element; an overlay element; a list view element; a radio button element; or a drop-down element. 12. The system as described in claim 10, wherein the user-interactive element detection module is configured to determine the plurality of user-interactive elements that are included in the web page by identifying, for each of the plurality of user-interactive elements, at least one of an HTML identifier attribute or a class name of the user-interactive element. 13. The system as described in claim 10, wherein the user-interactive element detection module is further configured to filter the plurality of user- interactive elements into multiple groups, each of the multiple groups including user-interactive elements of a common type, and the test generator module is configured to generate a single test script for each of the multiple groups. 14. The system as described in claim 10, wherein the threshold frame rendering rate is specified by a user of the computing device. 15. The system as described in claim 10, wherin the test generator module is configured to assign a unique test identifier to each generated test script and the rendering performance report includes information describing a rendering performance score for each unique test identifier. 16. The system as described in claim 10, wherein the rendering performance report includes an overall rendering performance score for the web page, the rendering performance score generated relative to the threshold frame rendering rate. 17. A computer-readable storage medium having instructions stored thereon that are executable by at least one processor of a computing device to perform operations comprising: identifying, by the at least one computing device, user-interactive elements that are included in a web page; simulating, by the at least one computing device, user interaction with the user-interactive elements and measuring a number of frames rendered in a timeframe during the simulating; comparing, by the at least one computing device, the measured number of frames rendered in the timeframe to a threshold frame rendering rate for the at least one computing device to determine whether the simulation of user interaction with the user interactive elements provides a satisfactory user experience in which the measured number of frames rendered in the timeframe satisfies the threshold frame rendering rate; and outputting, by the at least one computing device, a rendering performance report indicating whether the satisfactory user experience is provided. 18. The computer-readable storage medium as described in claim 17, wherein simulating user interaction with the user-interactive elements comprises simulating user interaction with the user-interactive elements at a first device type and simulating user interaction with the user-interactive elements at a second device type that is different than the first device type, wherein the rendering performance report includes information describing differences between simulating user interaction at the first and second device types. 19. The computer-readable storage medium as described in claim 17, wherein simulating user interaction with the user-interactive elements comprises simulating user interaction with the user-interactive elements under a first set of network conditions and simulating user interaction with the user-interactive elements under a second set of network conditions that are different than the first set of network conditions, wherein the rendering performance report includes information describing differences between simulating user interaction under the first and second sets of network conditions. 20. The computer-readable storage medium as described in claim 17, wherein identifying user-interactive elements that are included in a web page comprises scanning the web page and identifying each of the user-interactive elements based on at least one of an HTML identifier attribute or a class name of the user-interactive element.
